claim: Enhanced early production of inflammatory chemokines improves viral control in the lung.

result.claim: Enhanced early production of inflammatory chemokines improves viral control in the lung.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper 19005293, "Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen". The abstract of this paper explicitly states that "the response of memory, but not naive, CD4+ T cells enhances production of multiple innate inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (IICs) in the lung and that, during influenza infection, this leads to early control of virus." This directly aligns with the claim that enhanced early production of inflammatory chemokines improves viral control in the lung. While the paper's authors and year are unknown, and it has 0 citations, the directness of the statement provides strong support. No contradictory evidence was found in the initial search.

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cells enhance production of multiple innate inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (IICs) in the lung.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: Injection of B cell leukemia/lymphoma-2 protein (BCl-2)-transduced CX3CR1-deficient T(H)2 cells into CX3CR1-deficient mice restored asthma.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1-induced survival was also observed for T(H)1 cells upon airway inflammation.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: Transfer of WT CD4(+) T cells into CX3CR1-deficient mice restored the cardinal features of asthma.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: Untreated CX3CR1-deficient mice show reduced lung disease upon allergen sensitization and challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: Wild-type (WT) mice treated with CX3CR1-blocking reagents show reduced lung disease upon allergen sensitization and challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1 signaling promoted T(H)2 survival in the inflamed lungs.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1-blocking reagents prevented airway inflammation in CX3CR1-deficient recipients injected with WT T(H)2 cells.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CL1 expression is increased in airway smooth muscle, lung endothelium and epithelium upon allergen challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: During influenza infection, memory CD4+ T cells lead to early control of virus.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen
